artifacts.note: The native-event observation boundary transfers; Redact's synthetic onChange mapping is an explicit Octane divergence.
Host reconnecting outcomes transfer; class-component cases are explicit non-goals.
The cases reinforce streamed-boundary recovery and transactional streaming entries.
Redact-specific React aliases are excluded; option-to-output and environment-routing lessons transfer.

entries.title: Public options must change the emitted build at their observation boundary

sources.title: fix(redact/vite): respect 'forwardRef' and 'classComponents' feature flags

entries.symptom: A documented false-valued feature flag silently left the full implementation in the bundle because an internal folder name was cast to an unrelated public option key.
entries.octaneContract: Every public compiler or bundler boolean must have a test proving the option changes emitted code, resolution, bundle contents, or runtime behavior in each environment where it is supported.

octaneReferences.testName: hmr option off → no wrapping, no accept block
octaneReferences.note: Representative direct option-to-output coverage; not yet a complete public-option matrix.

octaneReferences.testName: erases profiling and full diagnostics from a real production bundle

nextAction.acceptance: Inventory public booleans and renderer mappings, then prove both enabled and disabled behavior at emitted-output or resolved-module boundaries without relying on unchecked name casts.

entries.rationale: Redact's forwardRef and class-component flags are out of scope; the transferable lesson is to test configuration where consumers observe it.

entries.title: Surface, protocol, and size documentation must follow executable evidence

sources.title: docs: update SURFACE.md and SAVINGS_ANALYSIS.md for @tanstack/redact
docs(redact/server): correct fallback wire-format comment — fallback '<div>' is visible, not hidden

entries.symptom: Published size numbers, package layout, or streaming wire-format commentary drifted away from the scripts and bytes that users actually received.
entries.octaneContract: Measured claims and protocol descriptions must cite the live generator, benchmark, emitted fixture, or focused test that makes them reviewable.

octaneReferences.note: Distinguishes current source-backed claims from historical plans.
Defines comparable baselines and the repository benchmark contract.

entries.rationale: This is a maintenance rule rather than an independent runtime workstream.

entries.title: Native form events must be exercised, without importing synthetic onChange

sources.title: fix(react-dom): fire onChange on every keystroke for text inputs

entries.symptom: Initial form markup looked correct while real editing was dead because the tests never dispatched the platform event that drives the control.
entries.octaneContract: Controlled-property tests must dispatch real native input/change events and assert handler delivery, live-property reassertion, and dynamic input-type behavior.

octaneReferences.note: Octane deliberately uses onInput per edit and native change on commit.

octaneReferences.testName: fires Octane change on focus commit while React derives it from each input
octaneReferences.note: Real-browser native change contract.

entries.rationale: Adopt Redact's event-level observation boundary, but reject its synthetic onChange-to-input mapping because that conflicts with Octane's documented native-event model.

entries.title: Hydration event replay preserves platform event shape and queue semantics

sources.testName: replays buffered events with browser-compatible event subclasses

entries.symptom: A replay queue delivered the right event names but degraded keyboard and input events to generic Event objects, lost target/default semantics, changed order, or replayed an item twice.
entries.octaneContract: For every event Octane buffers, replay must preserve the documented platform subclass and supported metadata, original target, FIFO order, bubbling/cancelability/default behavior, and exactly-once delivery.
entries.owner: Octane deferred hydration and DOM events

octaneReferences.note: Mouse and focus families retain subclasses; other buffered families currently fall back to Event.

octaneReferences.testName: replays pre-root interaction after the pending stream reveals
octaneReferences.note: Covers click delivery and target survival, not the cross-family event-shape matrix.

nextAction.acceptance: Exercise every public HydrationInteractionEvent family in a real browser: click/auxclick/contextmenu/dblclick, focusin, keydown/keyup, mouse, and pointer events. Assert subclass, supported metadata, target, FIFO ordering, preventDefault visibility, queue drain, and no duplicate replay; keep input/change/submit excluded unless a separate API decision expands the public union.

entries.rationale: Octane replays interaction intent rather than cloning every native event field, so the transferable contract must explicitly define which event shape it promises instead of silently copying Redact's implementation.

entries.title: Suspending hydration recovery must not leak an uncaught mismatch

sources.title: Hydration bugs: async-resume mismatch escapes uncaught; head-script claiming; innerHTML probe escaping; foreign-node strictness

sources.testName: recovers a mismatch when lazy hydration resumes inside Suspense

entries.symptom: A mismatch reached after a lazy hydration suspension reported as recoverable and then escaped through the global error channel, abandoning interaction setup.
entries.octaneContract: A client suspension encountered during hydration must converge to exactly one live client arm, report through Octane's chosen hydration channel only, preserve unaffected siblings, and never emit an uncaught error or rejection.
entries.owner: Octane runtime hydration and browser tests

octaneReferences.testName: reports eager pending-arm recovery instead of deferring a streamed-boundary mismatch
octaneReferences.note: Already proves one final client arm and one expected diagnostic in development and production compile modes.

nextAction.acceptance: Retain the existing one-final-arm oracle, add global error/unhandled-rejection capture, and prove an outside sibling keeps identity and remains interactive after resolution.

entries.rationale: Issue #17 remained open when captured, but Redact main commit e1620a13aab8935c806238f117ba58559b7cd002 contains the matching fix and regression, so the resolution is inferred rather than administratively confirmed. Octane eagerly leaves synchronous hydration for a fresh client arm, so Redact's resumed-cursor implementation bug does not transfer directly; only the missing global-error and outside-sibling observations remain.

entries.title: Hoisted head markers must only claim the intended element

sources.title: Hydration bugs: async-resume mismatch escapes uncaught; head-script claiming; innerHTML probe escaping; foreign-node strictness

sources.testName: claims typeless and typed head scripts in document order

entries.symptom: Weak head-node identity allowed one logical head entry to adopt a different server node and validate or update the wrong content.
entries.octaneContract: A compiled head marker may adopt only an element with the expected tag and ownership; missing, duplicated, reordered, or corrupted markers must create or recover the expected node without mutating unrelated head content.
entries.owner: Octane compiler and runtime head hydration

octaneReferences.note: The current helper returns the first element after a matching marker without validating the expected tag.

octaneReferences.testName: adopts the server head (one <title>/<meta>, markers removed) + single-root body, removed on unmount
octaneReferences.note: Happy-path adoption only.

nextAction.acceptance: Cover a foreign wrong-tag node between marker and target, missing target, duplicate marker, reordered metadata, and preservation of unrelated head nodes before deciding whether runtime validation is needed.

entries.rationale: Redact matches scripts by attributes while Octane uses compiler markers; the portable contract is ownership-safe claiming, not Redact's matching algorithm.

entries.title: Raw script and style hydration must use their parsing contexts

sources.title: Hydration bugs: async-resume mismatch escapes uncaught; head-script claiming; innerHTML probe escaping; foreign-node strictness

sources.testName: hydrates matching raw script content without HTML normalization

entries.symptom: Byte-equivalent script or style text containing ampersands or less-than signs was parsed through a generic div and falsely reported as mismatched.
entries.octaneContract: Hydration comparison must respect script-data and style raw-text parsing, preserve node identity for equivalent server bytes, avoid false diagnostics, and remain safe against closing-tag injection.
entries.owner: Octane DOM hydration and SSR serialization

octaneReferences.note: Script uses textContent; other hosts use a same-tag contextual probe.

octaneReferences.testName: hydrates the server-safe script spelling by adoption and applies later updates
octaneReferences.note: Script coverage is already strong.

octaneReferences.testName: keeps raw style text in one element when it contains closing-tag-like tokens
octaneReferences.note: Current style evidence is server-only.

nextAction.acceptance: Add server-render, parse, hydrate, and update coverage for raw style text containing &&, <, entity-like text, and closing-tag-like tokens; assert adoption and no warning in the unit lane, then use Chromium/CSSOM to prove intact stylesheet semantics.

entries.rationale: The Redact script failure is already prevented; the remaining gap is direct hydration evidence for style's distinct raw-text and SSR-escape behavior.

entries.title: Foreign document nodes do not weaken ordinary element-root ownership

sources.title: Hydration bugs: async-resume mismatch escapes uncaught; head-script claiming; innerHTML probe escaping; foreign-node strictness
Project document head elements

sources.testName: preserves foreign nodes after expected document body children

entries.symptom: A hidden iframe injected at the open document boundary caused whole-document hydration to fail, even though it was not application-owned content.
entries.octaneContract: The element passed to hydrateRoot remains strictly owned: ordinary unclaimed content inside it is diagnosed and removed, while ordinary nodes outside it are untouched. Explicit portal targets and compiler-owned head hoists follow their own ownership contracts. Document/body tolerance requires a separate explicit API and ownership decision.
entries.owner: Octane root API and metaframework integrations

octaneReferences.note: Intentionally diagnoses and removes unclaimed root siblings.

octaneReferences.testName: server renders an extra element the client omits (Per :834)

entries.rationale: Octane hydrates a supplied Element rather than Document. Redact's body-level tolerance must not become blanket permissiveness inside an application-owned container. Revisit only if Octane introduces document/body hydration.

entries.title: Hydration recovery reporting has an explicit public contract

sources.title: Hydration bugs: async-resume mismatch escapes uncaught; head-script claiming; innerHTML probe escaping; foreign-node strictness
fix(redact): harden document hydration recovery

entries.symptom: The same hydration failure appeared on both a recoverable callback and the global error channel, making recovery behavior ambiguous to framework integrations.
entries.octaneContract: Octane must document whether hydration diagnostics are console-only or root-callback-addressable and ensure every initial, deferred, and streamed recovery path follows that decision exactly once.

octaneReferences.note: Documents that onRecoverableError is not currently an Octane root option.

nextAction.acceptance: Choose and document console-only diagnostics versus recoverable/caught/uncaught root callbacks, including exact deduplication and production behavior, before adding an API.

entries.rationale: Redact's callback surface should not be copied implicitly; frameworks need a deliberate Octane contract if error callbacks become public.

entries.title: Mismatch recovery is bounded to the failed ownership scope

sources.testName: root fallback produces one clean client tree for wrong element type
Suspense-scoped extra server node recovery preserves siblings outside the boundary
Suspense-scoped missing server node recovery preserves siblings outside the boundary
Suspense-scoped recovery attaches events to the client-rendered subtree
nearest host recovery preserves siblings outside the failed host subtree

entries.symptom: Recovering one mismatched host or Suspense subtree could remount unaffected siblings, abandon the hydration cursor, or leave the regenerated subtree without live handlers.
entries.octaneContract: A structural hydration mismatch rebuilds only its owning failed range, preserves the object identity of unaffected siblings outside that range, and installs events on regenerated content.
entries.owner: Octane hydration cursor and ownership ranges

octaneReferences.testName: hydration continues past a mismatch: the next sibling adopts + is interactive
octaneReferences.note: Proves final content and sibling interactivity, but does not retain and compare the sibling object across the recovery.

octaneReferences.testName: PROD build: @if branch swap rebuilds SILENTLY (recovery is not gated on the dev loc)
octaneReferences.note: Proves production recovery but not a nearest-host/Suspense containment matrix.

nextAction.acceptance: For root, nearest-host, and Suspense-scoped structural mismatches, capture outside siblings before hydration and assert the same objects and handlers survive while the failed subtree is regenerated and its handlers become live in development and production.

entries.rationale: Octane recovers ranges in place instead of following Redact's checkpoint stack, so the transferable requirement is the observable containment boundary rather than checkpoint implementation parity.

entries.title: Each root owns a deterministic useId sequence

sources.title: fix(redact): harden document hydration recovery

sources.testName: starts hydrated useId sequences from each server-rendered root

entries.symptom: A prior root's allocations shifted the next root's hydrated IDs away from the server sequence.
entries.octaneContract: Sibling client roots are independently namespaced, server IDs hydrate byte-for-byte, and explicit identifier prefixes compose with root-local allocation.

octaneReferences.testName: automatically namespaces sibling createRoot roots
server useId is preserved byte-for-byte after hydrateRoot()

evidence.testName: automatically namespaces sibling createRoot roots
server useId is preserved byte-for-byte after hydrateRoot()
hydrates completed boundary useId values in its opaque stream namespace

entries.title: Coalesced dependency changes leave one live passive side effect

sources.testName: does not leak side-effects when two deps-changing renders coalesce before passive drain

entries.symptom: Two renders before the passive queue drained installed multiple imperative artifacts because cleanup ownership was cleared before the intermediate body ran.
entries.octaneContract: After any coalesced dependency-changing render sequence, at most one passive side effect is live; each installed effect is cleaned exactly once, including final unmount.

octaneReferences.testName: phase order on re-render: cleanups fire before bodies of same phase
octaneReferences.note: Strong ordering coverage, but not the exact two-renders-before-passive-drain race.

nextAction.acceptance: Drive a→b→c before passive drain, assert one live imperative artifact, exact cleanup order through c, and one final cleanup on unmount in development and production compile modes.

entries.title: Memo prop bailouts do not swallow owned updates or revive removed work

sources.testName: re-renders a memo component when its useSyncExternalStore fires, even with unchanged props
does not mount DOM when a pending fiber was unmounted between scheduling and flush

entries.symptom: A stable-prop memo gate swallowed the component's own store update, while stale queued work could render after an ancestor removed it.
entries.octaneContract: A memoized component processes its own state/store updates without disabling descendant memo bailouts, and pending work beneath a removed ancestor cannot mount new DOM.

octaneReferences.testName: should update children even if parent blocks updates
does not call render after a component as been deleted

nextAction.acceptance: Combine memo with useSyncExternalStore, prove the owner updates while a stable memo grandchild bails, then queue/remove work and prove no zombie DOM or effects appear.

entries.title: React package shims, RSC internals, and private debug names are not Octane targets

sources.title: fix(redact): scope resolve.alias to client+ssr envs so RSC stays on real React
Add React DOM edge server aliases
Expose ReactDOM Flight hint internals
chore(redact/hydration): rename internal '__tdom*' scroll-guard globals to '__redact*'
feat(redact): vinext SSR/hydration compatibility — 0.0.9

entries.symptom: Redact needed compatibility with React package names, Flight private internals, RSC environment routing, and legacy internal global names.
entries.octaneContract: Octane remains responsible for its public runtime, compiler, SSR, hydration, and documented bundler integrations, not for impersonating React private package or Flight surfaces.

octaneReferences.note: Records unsupported React-only surfaces.

entries.rationale: Class components, forwardRef, Server Components/Flight, React package aliases, Redact's renderer registry, and private debug globals are architecture-specific exclusions. Portable packaging or SSR lessons are tracked separately.

entries.title: Performance claims use real browsers and multiple controlled workloads

sources.title: perf(redact): close ~85% of the render-bench gap to React
perf(redact): four more render-bench wins — now ~18% faster than React

entries.symptom: JSDOM suggested the opposite performance result from real Chrome, and a single workload hid regressions in keyed reorder, mount/unmount, deep trees, or state churn.
entries.octaneContract: Framework performance changes require same-machine comparable baselines, semantic controls, real-browser measurement for DOM work, representative workload coverage, and bundle/codegen accounting.

octaneReferences.note: Requires relevant baselines and forbids unsupported performance claims.
Defines browser/Node runners, correctness gates, ratios, and workload inventory.

entries.rationale: Octane already follows this discipline. Add or run only the benchmark relevant to a future hot-path change; do not port Redact's optimizations.

entries.title: Advertised named exports are locked at the consumer boundary

sources.title: refactor!: rename to @tanstack/redact and consolidate into single package

sources.testName: exposes hooks needed by use-sync-external-store/shim/* aliases

entries.symptom: A hook existed in source but was omitted from the package entrypoint, producing a link-time failure only in a downstream consumer.
entries.octaneContract: Every intended value export for each public Octane subpath is checked from the built or packed artifact, so removing a re-export is an explicit review event rather than a downstream surprise.

octaneReferences.note: Proves every JS entry imports, but not that a specific named export remains present.
Builds isolated consumers from packed workspace artifacts.

nextAction.acceptance: Derive or explicitly declare intended named-export sets for public core subpaths and validate them from the built or packed artifact without making harmless additive exports unnecessarily brittle.

entries.title: Packed Vite consumers and supported integration graphs resolve one runtime

sources.title: refactor!: rename to @tanstack/redact and consolidate into single package
fix(redact): switch exports to `default` so RSC + other conditions resolve
fix(redact): scope resolve.alias to client+ssr envs so RSC stays on real React
Add React DOM edge server aliases
feat(redact): vinext SSR/hydration compatibility — 0.0.9

entries.symptom: Source-level tests passed while published export conditions or top-level aliases failed in a real framework environment or selected the wrong runtime graph.
entries.octaneContract: Outside-workspace consumers built from packed Octane artifacts resolve one runtime through Vite client and SSR graphs; supported Rspack and Rsbuild environment routing is exercised by their integration builds. This does not claim an external packed-consumer lane for every plugin.

octaneReferences.note: Published subpaths provide default conditions.
Packs, installs, resolves, builds, and executes consumers outside the workspace.

evidence.testName: discovers a parent package and routes raw dependency imports to the SSR runtime
builds client and server graphs with maps, raw dependencies, and one target runtime
builds routed client/server environments and serves the production SSR handler

entries.rationale: The exact React/RSC aliases are Redact-only. Octane's packed canary proves the external Vite boundary, while Rspack and Rsbuild remain honestly labeled workspace integration evidence rather than external packed coverage.

entries.title: Portal content mounts in its target and cleans up with its owner

sources.testName: mounts portal children into the supplied container
unmounts portal children when the parent stops rendering them
renders portal alongside sibling elements correctly

entries.symptom: Portal content mounted outside its supplied target or remained there after the logical owner stopped rendering it.
entries.octaneContract: Portal content mounts in the supplied foreign target, and closing or unmounting its logical owner removes that content.

octaneReferences.testName: compiles and renders an inline host-element body into the target, updating reactively

evidence.testName: compiles and renders an inline host-element body into the target, updating reactively
unmounts portal content when the if-branch closes

entries.title: A stateful portal descendant resolves its foreign host for owned updates

sources.testName: re-renders a Portal descendant when its own state updates (getHostParent on Portal)

entries.symptom: A component inside a portal scheduled its own state update, resolved the logical parent as its host, and inserted or replaced DOM outside the portal target.
entries.octaneContract: A descendant component rendered inside a portal may own and schedule updates while all of its host mutations remain anchored in the supplied portal target and preserve surrounding target siblings.

octaneReferences.testName: compiles and renders an inline host-element body into the target, updating reactively
octaneReferences.note: Updates state owned outside the portal, so it does not exercise descendant-owned host resolution.

nextAction.acceptance: Render a child component with its own useState inside a portal, schedule its update from that child, and assert the same target and unaffected target siblings retain identity while only the child's host content changes in development and production compile modes.

entries.title: Keyed reorders preserve survivor identity and final order

sources.testName: reorders keyed list items preserving DOM identity

entries.symptom: A keyed reorder produced the right text but recreated survivor DOM nodes or left them in the wrong final order.
entries.octaneContract: Keyed survivors retain object identity and reach the requested final order; Octane's LIS-selected physical move set need not match React or Redact.

octaneReferences.testName: reverse preserves all instances (pure moves)

octaneReferences.note: Exercises prefix, tail, small-displacement, and LIS paths across deterministic mutation streams.

evidence.testName: reverse preserves all instances (pure moves)
inserting in the middle preserves existing instances

entries.rationale: Physical move-sequence parity is intentionally excluded because Octane's LIS reconciler promises final order and survivor identity with fewer moves.

entries.title: Topology transitions use the correct absolute anchor without stable reattachment

sources.testName: Sidebar Portal→div via Provider cascade: new div lands BEFORE chat
first child swaps Portal→div with multiple later siblings
first child goes null→div via Provider cascade: new div at index 0
stable re-render does not reorder stable DOM

entries.symptom: A Portal-to-element or null-to-element transition inserted new output after the wrong sibling, while a no-op rerender could detach and reinsert otherwise stable DOM.
entries.octaneContract: When a child changes topology, each newly materialized range lands at its absolute logical anchor around portal and ordinary siblings; a stable rerender emits no child-list mutations and preserves every existing host object.

octaneReferences.note: Covers final source order across control-flow transitions, but differential HTML cannot see host reattachment and has no portal topology case.

octaneReferences.testName: unmounts portal content when the if-branch closes
octaneReferences.note: Covers removal/recreation, not Portal→ordinary replacement around stable siblings.

nextAction.acceptance: Port Portal→element and null→element first-child transitions with multiple stable later siblings; retain every survivor object, assert exact final order, and use MutationObserver in Chromium to prove a stable rerender produces no child-list records.

entries.rationale: The existing keyed identity suite proves a different reconciler contract. Redact's topology cases require portal-aware absolute placement plus a mutation-level oracle, so they remain an explicit gap instead of being hidden under RDX-REC-001.

entries.title: Callback refs run in commit order and clean up exactly once

sources.testName: does not invoke a callback ref during render
runs the cleanup ref(null) on unmount
runs the user-provided cleanup function returned from a callback ref

entries.symptom: A callback ref fired during render or received both its returned cleanup and a redundant null detach call.
entries.octaneContract: Refs attach only after connected DOM exists, precede layout-effect bodies, detach in the documented commit order, and use either cleanup-return or legacy null notification exactly once.

octaneReferences.testName: ref attaches before the layout effect body (mount), layout cleanup before ref detach (unmount)
handles detaching refs with either cleanup function or null argument

evidence.testName: a callback ref fires with a node already connected to the document
ref attaches before the layout effect body (mount), layout cleanup before ref detach (unmount)
handles detaching refs with either cleanup function or null argument

entries.title: The first root render clears foreign children exactly once

sources.testName: clears pre-existing children on the first render
does not re-clear on subsequent renders (preserves reconciled DOM)

entries.symptom: A root either left stale foreign children in its managed container or cleared the entire container again on an update, destroying reconciled host identity.
entries.octaneContract: The first createRoot render removes pre-existing unowned children before mounting the client tree; later renders reconcile that managed tree without clearing the container and preserve stable host objects.

octaneReferences.testName: clears existing children
should reuse markup if rendering to the same target twice

evidence.testName: clears existing children
should reuse markup if rendering to the same target twice

entries.title: Untrusted host values remain inside their parser and execution boundaries

sources.testName: escapes <, >, & in text children
escapes quotes in attribute values
breaks an attempted </script> inside script body
breaks an attempted </style> inside style body
does not allow CSS injection via style values containing quotes
only attaches function event handlers — string handlers are silently ignored
only honors the `__html` key — extra keys are ignored

entries.symptom: Untrusted text, attributes, styles, raw-text bodies, event props, or malformed raw-HTML objects crossed into executable markup or a neighboring parser context.
entries.octaneContract: Untrusted values remain within their owning text, attribute, style, or raw-text parser context at each tested serialization boundary; streamed raw script and style bodies remain confined as chunks are emitted. Raw HTML requires the __html shape, and non-function delegated event values never execute as handlers.
entries.owner: Octane DOM application and server serialization

octaneReferences.testName: escapes text content and attribute values (round-trip)
keeps valid inline script characters while preventing closing-tag injection
a $label listener warns at render and reports an Error at dispatch

evidence.testName: escapes text content and attribute values (round-trip)
keeps valid inline script characters while preventing closing-tag injection
keeps raw style text in one element when it contains closing-tag-like tokens
a $label listener warns at render and reports an Error at dispatch
throws for a malformed dangerouslySetInnerHTML value

entries.rationale: Octane follows React's whole-inline-script escape rather than Redact's exact comment/token spelling, reports invalid listeners instead of silently ignoring them, and rejects malformed raw HTML instead of ignoring extra shapes. The parser/execution containment is covered; byte-level Redact parity is intentionally not claimed.

entries.title: SSR retries and render replays are transactional and request-scoped

sources.title: Support vinext SSR stream bootstrap
feat(redact): vinext SSR/hydration compatibility — 0.0.9

sources.testName: restores nested providers
retries when the root render suspends before any boundary
hydrates a resolved boundary against the streamed real content
re-hydrates a pending boundary when the server reveal fires

entries.symptom: A root-level suspension or boundary replay could leak partial chunks, IDs, boundary records, or provider values into the retry or another request.
entries.octaneContract: Buffered render replays and streamed retries isolate render state, retire discarded boundary records, preserve provider stacks and stable IDs, hydrate revealed ranges in place, and report terminal root failure once.
entries.owner: Octane server renderer and streaming transports

octaneReferences.note: Covers discarded passes, stable IDs, retired boundaries, and late errors.
Covers stream/hydration interleavings, reveal replacement, failure reporting, and seed scopes.
Bootstrap modules/import maps remain a metaframework concern rather than a core renderer option.

evidence.testName: settled output is byte-identical to a single-pass render of the final state (useId, markers, sibling order)
rewinds the suspense seed stream — one use() seeds exactly once across the passes
restores each Provider value across suspended concurrent streams
delivers a boundary that resolves while a bare Promise delays the shell
does not retain a boundary registered by a discarded render-phase pass
swaps the segment into place, scopes its seeds, and hydrates byte-for-byte
reports a root failure through the shell callbacks and rejects a readable stream

entries.rationale: Redact's inline bootstrap API belongs at Octane's plugin/adapter boundary; the portable transactional retry and request-isolation contracts are already strongly covered.

entries.title: Doctype ownership is explicit for each server rendering API

sources.testName: prepends a DOCTYPE when the root element is <html>
does not prepend a DOCTYPE for non-html roots

entries.symptom: A document response omitted or misplaced its doctype and entered quirks mode, or a fragment unexpectedly received a document preamble.
entries.octaneContract: Pipeable <html> document streams contain exactly one <!DOCTYPE html>, and readable <html> streams begin with that doctype; the shared serializer emits none for non-document roots, directly exercised through the pipeable API; renderToString and renderToStaticMarkup remain doctype-free so the embedding framework owns buffered preambles.

octaneReferences.note: Documents streaming-only doctype ownership and buffered framework composition.
Covers injected/non-injected document streams, fragments, web streams, and buffered APIs.

evidence.testName: streamed documents lead with <!DOCTYPE html> without any injection source
streamed documents lead with <!DOCTYPE html> through the web-stream API
streamed fragments never receive a doctype
buffered renderers stay doctype-free for document roots, matching React

entries.rationale: Redact's buffered renderer prepends a doctype for an <html> root. Octane intentionally matches React's API split: streaming owns the document preamble, while buffered output remains composable. The transferable contract is explicit API ownership, not identical bytes across unlike APIs.

entries.title: External-store subscription races cannot create zombie DOM

sources.testName: subscribes after render when the store synchronously notifies
store notifications after unmount do not resurrect DOM

entries.symptom: Subscription ran during render or a stale listener scheduled an unmounted component, causing removed UI to reappear in its former host.
entries.octaneContract: useSyncExternalStore subscribes after render, converges when subscribe synchronously notifies, unsubscribes on conditional/root removal, and ignores every post-unmount notification.

octaneReferences.testName: post-unmount store mutations do NOT trigger any work
octaneReferences.note: Covers root unmount but not synchronous subscribe notification or conditional stale-listener delivery.

nextAction.acceptance: Prove subscription occurs outside render, a synchronous notifier converges without looping, conditional removal unsubscribes, and a retained stale callback cannot resurrect DOM.

entries.title: Re-suspension preserves browser-owned state in committed UI

sources.title: fix(redact): preserve committed DOM across Suspense re-suspension

sources.testName: preserves focus on an input across a sibling suspension

entries.symptom: Navigating through a suspension destroyed a committed sidebar, resetting scroll and focus even though the logical UI survived.
entries.octaneContract: An already committed Suspense primary tree preserves node identity, component state, scroll, focus, selection, uncontrolled values, and other browser-owned state through urgent and transitioned re-suspension, while refs/effects follow Octane's documented lifecycle.
entries.owner: Octane Suspense runtime and browser tests

octaneReferences.note: Ordinary Suspense currently parks committed DOM in savedDom before reinsertion.

octaneReferences.testName: detaches host refs on suspend and re-attaches on reveal
octaneReferences.note: Proves reuse of the same node, not browser focus/scroll preservation.

octaneReferences.testName: destroys the committed layout effect on re-suspend, recreates on reveal

nextAction.acceptance: In real Chromium, cover same-tree and route-swap re-suspension with identity, scrollTop, activeElement, selection, uncontrolled value, state, refs, and effect logs for urgent and transitioned paths before changing the hiding mechanism.

entries.rationale: The observable preservation contract transfers; Redact's display:none and detached-fallback fiber implementation does not. JSDOM is insufficient for the browser-owned state assertions.
